results.title: God or Politics: Tom Keneally in Eritrea
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Creative Spirits Series”.
This documentary follows Tom Keneally as he retraces the journey made when he researched the novel “Towards Amsara” two years ago. The novel is set in a war-zone amongst the Eritrean people as they struggle to regain the independence taken from them by the Ethiopians twenty five years ago. Keneally reveals a remarkable society comprised of camouflaged schools and hospitals, factories hidden in deep caves, great food distribution dumps and ravaged biblical towns. He encounters again the real people he turned into fictional characters in his book.

results.title: Wonders of the deep
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World in action”.
A record of research and achievement in the technique of underwater photography. Brings to the screen new secrets of the sea and shows the methods by which cine film can be taken in the ocean at depths down to 100 feet by a mobile cameraman wearing self-contained diving equipment.

results.title: VD: don't take the risk
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World in action”
Statistics show that venereal disease rates are rising, particularly among teenagers; teenagers explain how they become affected, and later describe their experiences in clinics. A lecture, sensibly frank and usefully informative in respect to symptoms, is heard and discussed by a mixed sixth form; a doctor gives further information about the spread of the disease and clinical procedure.
Follow-up to previous Granada program “The Shadow of ignorance: an inquiry”.

results.title: Difficult pleasures: Brett Whiteley
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Creative Spirits Series”.
This is a revealing film about one of Australia’s leading painters - Brett Whiteley. Whiteley takes us into his studio where we catch glimpses of his free ranging talent. We see him at a huge canvas as he creates and completes a work during the film. He talks of being born with a gift and his desire to test and abuse that gift, but ultimately share it; as well as the influences in his life, Van Gogh and Francis Bacon and his preoccupation with the sensual.

results.title: Astonish me: Graeme Murphy
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Creative Spirits Series”.
An examination of Australia’s leading choreographer and founder of the internationally acclaimed Sydney Dance Company. In exploring the background to his creativity, Graeme returns to Mathinna, Tasmania for the first time in thirty years. There he talks about his childhood adventures, his inspiration for dance and his early days at the Australian Ballet.

results.title: The Wizard from Oz - James Morrison
results.creator_credit: John Ossher, Don Featherstone and Don Featherstone Productions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Creative Spirits Series”.
A personal perspective on the life and music of jazz multi-instrumentalist, James Morrison. He heads us through the documentary in a series of statements derived from interviews at a variety of locations - from an empty jazz club in the afternoon, to a studio in New York, seated among some of the finest international jazz musicians of the last fifty years. We encounter his family, his musical colleagues, friends and critics and learn about Jazz and about James Morrison: musician, composer, dare-devil and philosopher, and we listen to his music.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Ossher, Don Featherstone and Don Featherstone Productions

results.title: Back injury
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Your life in their hands”.
Many people suffer from back pain at some stage of their lives. This film studies the option that one man chose in order to try to relieve his pain.

results.title: The Trials of Zanzibar
results.creator_credit: Alan Segal, Stephen Clarke and Granada International Productions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World in action”
Reports on political trials being held in Zanzibar where the accused are refused defence counsel and the prosecution admits that some of the evidence was obtained under torture. Includes an interview with Zanzibar’s Attorney-General and Aboud Jumbe, Zanzibar’s Head of State
